title: WAI skill sync gap — templates/commands/ not auto-synced to .claude/commands/ between shipits
signal: templates/commands/wai.md had a full pre-check section (trust hook output, skip redundant file reads) but .claude/commands/wai.md was 370 lines behind — the installed version had never been synced. Every session wasted ~15-18k message tokens on redundant WAI-State.json reads, lug scans, and teaching scans that the session-start hook already computed. The sync gap went undetected for multiple sessions because the behavior difference (extra tool calls) isn't visible as an error.
rationale: Shipit syncs .claude/commands/ from templates/commands/ — but this spoke hadn't run shipit in a while. The gap caused measurable token waste every session. Any spoke that hasn't run shipit recently has this problem. Recommendation: session-start hook should detect out-of-sync .claude/commands/ files and surface a warning.
